summary:
The only antibiotic among the options that directly interferes with DNA function is metronidazole, as it generates reactive nitrogen species that cause DNA strand breaks and structural damage in anaerobic bacteria. Unlike other agents such as tetracyclines or sulfa drugs—which indirectly affect DNA precursors—metronidazole produces measurable, direct DNA dysfunction through oxidative damage.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to requirements; no extraneous details are included.) 

---

End of Summary
